# Student Management System — SQL Project

<div align="center">

![MySQL](https://img.shields.io/badge/MySQL-Database-blue?style=flat-square&logo=mysql)
![Tables](https://img.shields.io/badge/Tables-7-green?style=flat-square)
![SQL](https://img.shields.io/badge/SQL-Complete-orange?style=flat-square)

A complete relational database project built with **MySQL**, covering student records, faculty, courses, enrollments, attendance, and grades.

</div>

---

## 📁 Project Structure

```
student-management/
├── students.SQL          ← Main SQL file (DDL + DML + All Queries)
├── tables/
│   ├── DEPARTMENTS.png
│   ├── STUDENTS.png
│   ├── Faculty.png
│   ├── Courses.png
│   ├── Enrollments.png
│   ├── Attendance.png
│   └── Grades.png
└── README.md
```

---

## 🗄️ Database Tables

### 🏢 1. Departments

> Stores all engineering department names.

| Column | Type | Constraint |
|--------|------|------------|
| department_id | INT | PRIMARY KEY |
| department_name | VARCHAR(50) | |

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Departments Table" style="width:100%;border-radius:6px;border:1px solid #30363d;display:block;">

---

### 🎓 2. Students

> Stores student personal and academic details.

| Column | Type | Constraint |
|--------|------|------------|
| student_id | INT | PRIMARY KEY |
| name | VARCHAR(50) | |
| dob | DATE | |
| gender | VARCHAR(10) | |
| email | VARCHAR(100) | |
| phone_number | VARCHAR(15) | |
| address | VARCHAR(100) | |
| admission_date | DATE | |
| department_id | INT | FK → Departments |

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Students Table" style="width:100%;border-radius:6px;border:1px solid #30363d;display:block;">

---

### 👨‍🏫 3. Faculty

> Stores faculty contact and department information.

| Column | Type | Constraint |
|--------|------|------------|
| faculty_id | INT | PRIMARY KEY |
| name | VARCHAR(50) | |
| email | VARCHAR(100) | |
| phone_number | VARCHAR(15) | |
| department_id | INT | FK → Departments |

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Faculty Table" style="width:100%;border-radius:6px;border:1px solid #30363d;display:block;">

---

### 📚 4. Courses

> Maps each course to a faculty member.

| Column | Type | Constraint |
|--------|------|------------|
| course_id | INT | PRIMARY KEY |
| course_name | VARCHAR(50) | |
| faculty_id | INT | FK → Faculty |

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Courses Table" style="width:100%;border-radius:6px;border:1px solid #30363d;display:block;">

---

### 📝 5. Enrollments

> Tracks which students are enrolled in which courses.

| Column | Type | Constraint |
|--------|------|------------|
| enrollment_id | INT | PRIMARY KEY |
| student_id | INT | FK → Students |
| course_id | INT | FK → Courses |
| enrollment_date | DATE | |
| | | UNIQUE(student_id, course_id) |

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Enrollments Table" style="width:100%;border-radius:6px;border:1px solid #30363d;display:block;">

---

### 📅 6. Attendance

> Tracks daily attendance status per student per course.

| Column | Type | Constraint |
|--------|------|------------|
| attendance_id | INT | PRIMARY KEY |
| student_id | INT | FK → Students |
| course_id | INT | FK → Courses |
| attendance_date | DATE | |
| status | VARCHAR(10) | Present / Absent / Late |

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Attendance Table" style="width:100%;border-radius:6px;border:1px solid #30363d;display:block;">

---

### 🏆 7. Grades

> Stores marks and letter grades per student per course.

| Column | Type | Constraint |
|--------|------|------------|
| grade_id | INT | PRIMARY KEY |
| student_id | INT | FK → Students |
| course_id | INT | FK → Courses |
| marks_obtained | INT | |
| grade | VARCHAR(2) | A / B / C / D |

<img src="data:image/png;base64," alt="Grades Table" style="width:100%;border-radius:6px;border:1px solid #30363d;display:block;">

---

## 🔗 Entity Relationship

```
Departments
├── Students        (FK: department_id)
└── Faculty         (FK: department_id)
        └── Courses (FK: faculty_id)
                ├── Enrollments  (FK: student_id + course_id)
                ├── Attendance   (FK: student_id + course_id)
                └── Grades       (FK: student_id + course_id)
```

---

## 📋 SQL Queries

### 1. CRUD Operations

```sql
-- INSERT new student
INSERT INTO Students VALUES
(11,'Rohit','2003-01-01','Male',NULL,'9999999999','Surat','2023-06-01',1);

-- UPDATE phone number
UPDATE Students SET phone_number = '8888888888' WHERE student_id = 1;

-- DELETE student
DELETE FROM Students WHERE student_id = 11;
```

### 2. WHERE · HAVING · LIMIT

```sql
-- Students in Computer Science
SELECT s.* FROM Students s
JOIN Departments d ON s.department_id = d.department_id
WHERE d.department_name = 'Computer Science';

-- Top 10 highest scoring students
SELECT * FROM Grades ORDER BY marks_obtained DESC LIMIT 10;

-- Attendance below 75%
SELECT student_id,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN status='Present' THEN 1 END)*100/COUNT(*) AS attendance_pct
FROM Attendance
GROUP BY student_id
HAVING COUNT(CASE WHEN status='Present' THEN 1 END)*100/COUNT(*) < 75;
```

### 3. Operators — AND · OR · NOT

```sql
-- Marks > 90 OR marked Present
SELECT DISTINCT s.student_id FROM Students s
LEFT JOIN Grades g ON s.student_id = g.student_id
LEFT JOIN Attendance a ON s.student_id = a.student_id
WHERE g.marks_obtained > 90 OR a.status = 'Present';

-- Faculty NOT assigned to any course
SELECT * FROM Faculty
WHERE faculty_id NOT IN (SELECT faculty_id FROM Courses);
```

### 4. GROUP BY · Aggregate Functions

```sql
-- Count students per department
SELECT department_id, COUNT(*) AS total_students
FROM Students GROUP BY department_id;

-- Average marks per course
SELECT course_id, AVG(marks_obtained) AS avg_marks
FROM Grades GROUP BY course_id;

-- Highest & lowest marks per course
SELECT course_id, MAX(marks_obtained) AS highest, MIN(marks_obtained) AS lowest
FROM Grades GROUP BY course_id;
```

### 5. JOINs

```sql
-- INNER JOIN: Students with department names
SELECT s.name, d.department_name
FROM Students s INNER JOIN Departments d ON s.department_id = d.department_id;

-- LEFT JOIN: Students NOT enrolled in any course
SELECT s.* FROM Students s
LEFT JOIN Enrollments e ON s.student_id = e.student_id
WHERE e.student_id IS NULL;

-- FULL OUTER via UNION
SELECT s.student_id, g.grade FROM Students s
LEFT JOIN Grades g ON s.student_id = g.student_id
UNION
SELECT s.student_id, g.grade FROM Students s
RIGHT JOIN Grades g ON s.student_id = g.student_id;
```

### 6. Subqueries

```sql
-- Marks above class average
SELECT * FROM Grades
WHERE marks_obtained > (SELECT AVG(marks_obtained) FROM Grades);
```

### 7. Date Functions

```sql
SELECT MONTH(attendance_date) AS month FROM Attendance;
SELECT student_id, YEAR(CURDATE()) - YEAR(admission_date) AS years FROM Students;
SELECT DATE_FORMAT(attendance_date,'%d-%m-%Y') FROM Attendance;
```

### 8. String Functions

```sql
SELECT UPPER(name) FROM Faculty;
SELECT TRIM(name) FROM Students;
SELECT IFNULL(email, 'Email Not Provided') FROM Students;
```

### 9. Window Functions

```sql
-- Rank students by marks
SELECT student_id, marks_obtained,
  RANK() OVER (ORDER BY marks_obtained DESC) AS rank_no
FROM Grades;

-- Cumulative attendance per student
SELECT student_id,
  COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY student_id) AS total_attendance
FROM Attendance;
```

### 10. CASE Expressions

```sql
-- Student performance category
SELECT student_id, marks_obtained,
  CASE
    WHEN marks_obtained > 90 THEN 'Excellent'
    WHEN marks_obtained BETWEEN 75 AND 90 THEN 'Good'
    ELSE 'Needs Improvement'
  END AS performance
FROM Grades;

-- Attendance regularity
SELECT student_id,
  CASE
    WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN status='Present' THEN 1 END)*100/COUNT(*) > 80 THEN 'Regular'
    WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN status='Present' THEN 1 END)*100/COUNT(*) BETWEEN 50 AND 80 THEN 'Irregular'
    ELSE 'Defaulter'
  END AS category
FROM Attendance GROUP BY student_id;
```

---

## 📌 Concepts Covered

| Concept | Details |
|---------|---------|
| DDL | CREATE TABLE, PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, UNIQUE |
| DML | INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE |
| Clauses | WHERE, HAVING, LIMIT, ORDER BY, GROUP BY |
| Operators | AND, OR, NOT, IN, NOT IN |
| Joins | INNER, LEFT, RIGHT, FULL OUTER (UNION) |
| Aggregates | COUNT, AVG, MAX, MIN |
| Subqueries | Scalar & correlated |
| Date Functions | MONTH(), YEAR(), CURDATE(), DATE_FORMAT() |
| String Functions | UPPER(), TRIM(), IFNULL() |
| Window Functions | RANK(), COUNT() OVER PARTITION BY |
| CASE Expressions | Conditional logic inside SELECT |

---

## ⚙️ How to Run

```bash
mysql -u root -p
SOURCE /path/to/students.SQL;
```

Or open **MySQL Workbench** → run `students.SQL` top to bottom.

---

*Student Management System · MySQL · 7 Tables · 10 SQL Concepts*
